page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfr7.8xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated physical cores and 32 vCPUs using the Intel Xeon 8369HB processor, which operates at a clock speed of 3.3 GHz. The instance is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 10 Gbps.

In benchmark testing, the ecs.hfr7.8xlarge demonstrates average performance across most compute and memory metrics, with a notable strength in write operations. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance is measured at 1571.09, while the multi-core CPU performance reaches 33012.14. Memory bandwidth testing shows a read-only small block cached speed of 1856.97 GB/sec and a large block uncached read speed of 165.51 GB/sec. Notably, the large block uncached write bandwidth of 93.48 GB/sec ranks in the top 25% tier. Single-threaded gzip compression and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression yield throughputs of 26.77 GB/sec and 125.32 GB/sec, respectively.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated CPU allocation and high memory density provide predictable performance for memory-bound workloads without the overhead of hyperthreading, as the instance utilizes a 1.0 thread-per-core configuration. However, the lack of local NVMe storage requires reliance on network storage, which may introduce latency tradeoffs for I/O-heavy applications. The hardware profile and benchmark results make this instance suitable for memory-intensive applications, database hosting, enterprise applications, and memory-cached web serving.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfr7.8xlarge is an x86_64 memory-optimized server featuring 32 dedicated Intel Xeon 8369HB physical cores running at 3.3 GHz, paired with 256.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, this instance provides an 8.0 GB per core ratio with no local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmark data indicates average CPU and read memory bandwidth performance, but highlights a strong uncached write memory bandwidth of 93.48 GB/sec, placing it in the top 25% tier. The dedicated core allocation ensures consistent compute delivery. Lacking local storage, it relies on network storage over a 10 Gbps baseline network. This configuration is suitable for memory-intensive enterprise applications, database workloads, and caching servers.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfr7.8xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 32 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 256 GB RAM, and 10 Gbps network bandwidth, showing strong uncached write memory bandwidth in benchmarks.

tagline: A memory-optimized server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon cores and strong uncached write memory bandwidth for demanding enterprise workloads.
